observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare and take up the necessary adjusting entries in the books and debit to “Due from Local Government Units” account instead of the account Due from LGUs pursuant to COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015.
observations.reason: Transfers or aids to component barangays were still recorded as outright expenses in the books.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the recipient barangays to liquidate the funds by submitting the documentary requirements prescribed under Section 3.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.
observations.reason: The recipient barangays did not submit the liquidation report on the fund transferred by the Municipality as assistance to the frontliners.

observations.recommendation: We therefore recommended that Management continue to ensure that essential emergency medicines are procured at the most advantageous price for the government based on the existing data of DOH or other related agencies in accordance with Section 5 of GPPB Circular No. 01-2020 dated April 6, 2020.
observations.reason: The prices of some medical supplies exceeded the suggested ceiling price issued by the DOH as discussed in finding no. 11, Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management submit the Authority for the Entitlement of the Hazard Pay in accordance with Section 4.3 DBM Budget Circular No. 2020-1 dated March 24, 2020 for us to ascertain the propriety and validity of the claim. Further, we recommended that Management require the refund of the daily hazard pay granted to seven personnel who did not physically report for work on certain dates during the quarantine period.
observations.reason: Management did not submit proof of payment of refund from personnel who did not physically report for work on certain dates during the quarantine period in CY 2020.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our audit recommendation that the Management require the Agricultural Technologists to submit proof of actual exposure to specific hazards and occupational risks, in accordance with DBM-DOH Joint Circular No. 1 series of 2016. We further recommended that Management submit to the Auditor the Certification from the Department of Health to attest that the Agricultural Technologists are considered PHWs.
observations.reason: The Agricultural Technologists were still granted hazard pay in the absence of proof of actual exposure to specific hazards and certification from the DOH to attest that they are PHWs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management prepare a one-time report to the National Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council, through the Office of the Civil Defense, on all donations received by the Municipality and furnish the Auditor a copy of the (1) Acknowledgement Receipts of the Donations In-Kind; (2) Proof of receipt by and distribution to the beneficiaries; and (3) Inventory of remaining undistributed items, if any, in accordance with Section 1 of COA Circular No. 2020-009 dated April 21, 2020.
observations.reason: A report on the in-kind donations received by the Municipality was not furnished by the Management to the Auditor. Likewise, a copy of the distribution sheets or any equivalent documents were not submitted to the Auditor.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management refrain from procuring goods through reimbursement and to make payments by check directly to suppliers in order to withhold the taxes due the government pursuant to the pertinent provisions of Revenue Regulations Nos. 2-98 and 16-2005 dated April 17, 1998 and September 1, 2005, respectively.
observations.reason: Post-audit of the DVs in CY 2021 disclosed that there were disbursements made through reimbursement resulting in non-withholding of taxes from these government purchases.

observations.recommendation: We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant provide disclosures on the face of the SCBAA and/or the Notes to FS on the changes between the original and final budgets and reconciliation of actual amounts against the SCBAA and FS. Also reconcile records each month to avoid accumulation of reconciling items and facilitate the reconciliation of balances between the SCBAA and FS.
observations.reason: Examination of the NFS disclosed that an explanation of the changes between the original and final budget in the SCBAA as well as the reconciliation of actual amounts against the Statement of Financial Performance were not disclosed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer provide the Municipal Accountant a copy of the RPT records for CY 2018 and prior years to establish the beginning balance for CY 2019. Henceforth, furnish the Municipal Accountant at the beginning of each year a certified list of delinquent taxpayers with the amount due and collectible for the current year as the basis for recording RPT and SET Receivables in compliance with Section 20 of the NGAS Manual, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: The accounting staff informed the Audit Team that RPT/SET Receivables were taken up in the books of accounts based on the Municipal Treasurer.
observations.reason: Examination of the trial balances as of December 31, 2021 disclosed that the tax receivables were nil. As of December 31, 2021, the delinquent tax payers based on the records of the Municipal Treasurer amounted to ₱17,089,698.55.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LDRRM Council pass a resolution reverting the CYs 2011 to 2013 LDRRMF unspent balances to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund, for adoption by the Sangguniang Bayan through the Local Development Council. We further recommended that the members of the Local Finance Committee prepare the necessary transfer of funds and accounting reports to facilitate said reversion and certify the availability of said funds for subsequent reappropriation for social services.
observations.reason: The unexpended balances for CYs 2014 and 2015 amounting to ₱1,836,718.63 and ₱2,301,432.30, respectively, were still not reverted to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management ensure that the PPAs are aligned with GAD-related undertakings so that the intended beneficiaries are assured of the services, protection, and other assistance that may be provided, in order that the fund be used for the objective for which it was created.
observations.reason: There were still expenditures appropriated in the GAD Fund, such as financial assistance to senior citizens as well as medical and hospitalization subsidy programs, among others, with a total appropriation of ₱7,670,000.00.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant coordinate with the MPDC and MSWDO in tracing and locating documents to support the claims. We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant issue demand letters to concerned individuals, determine its collectability and, if warranted, request for write-off from the Commission on Audit.
observations.reason: The accounts remained dormant and no authority to write-off was requested from COA. As of December 31, 2021, the accounts remained dormant and no identification was made.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant see to it that: (a) the grant of the initial cash advance for the PCF is supported with authority by the Local Chief Executive and an estimate of expenses; (b) replenishment of the cash advance is made only when the disbursements reach at least 75%, or as the need requires, submitting a replenishment voucher supported by PCVs and other documents duly summarized in a report of disbursement; and (c) the PCF is not used for regular expenses, such as rentals, subscriptions, utilities and the like.
observations.reason: The rules and regulations on PCF were still not complied with. At year end, the petty cash fund was not fully liquidated and some documentary requirements on the granting of PCF were not attached to the DV. A balance of ₱208,296.87 was still not liquidated contrary to Section 48 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Vol. I.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management ensure that the LCPC performs its functions in accordance with DILG MC No. 2005-07 dated February 1, 2005 and submit to the Auditor the Records or Minutes of Meetings and Accomplishment Report, which are indicators that the LCPC is operational or functional.
observations.reason: The LCPC did not submit the Records or Minutes of Meetings and Accomplishment Report, hence, we could not validate whether it is fully operational.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Inventory Committee to conduct the physical count of properties annually. We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer reconcile their records with the results of the actual count.
observations.reason: A physical count was not conducted. There was no official document creating an Inventory Committee.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management stop the payment of honoraria to personnel not authorized under the Compensation Policy Guidelines No. 98-1 dated March 23, 1998. We further recommended that the concerned BIR personnel, including the approving officials, refund the honoraria received for lack of legal basis.
observations.reason: The ND remained unsettled as of December 31, 2021 despite the demand made by the Municipal Accountant from the BIR personnel.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive, together with the Sanggunian, require the refund of the excess amount granted and discontinue the granting of PEI in excess of the allowable maximum rate of ₱5,000.00 per personnel, as provided for in Section 1.a of E.O. No. 80 dated July 20, 2012, to avoid overpayment.
observations.reason: Appeal was submitted to COA Regional Office No. VII.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Inventory Committee include the count of supplies and materials when it conducts its actual physical count and that the Accounting and Municipal Treasurer maintain required supply ledger cards and stock cards, respectively, to comply with the prescribed procedures in the preparation of the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI). Furthermore, the Municipal Accountant shall strictly follow the Perpetual Method of recording its inventory, as provided in the NGAS Manual and recognize inventory accounts for the above supplies immediately recorded as expense.
observations.reason: Supplies and materials were still recorded as direct expenses because of the lack of manpower to handle the account and the unavailability of storage space.

observations.recommendation: We required the immediate refund in full of the amounts received in excess of what is authorized in DBM Budget Circular No. 2010-3 totaling ₱1,260,000.00, otherwise, a Notice of Disallowance shall be issued in this regard. Henceforth, the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer shall be cognizant of the pertinent provisions of law on the grant of allowances/incentives and not to certify disbursements which run counter to the intents and purposes of the regulations prescribed by concerned government agencies.
observations.reason: An appeal was submitted to COA Regional Office No. VII.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to create an
Inventory Committee to complete the physical count and direct the Accounting
and Property Units to reconcile the results of the physical count with the related
property and accounting records in accordance with the pertinent provisions of
COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020, to ensure the accuracy of
the reported PPE balances in the financial statements. Any discrepancies noted
in the reconciliation shall be adjusted in accordance with Section 7.0 and 8.0 of
the Circular as a one-time cleansing of the PPE account balances.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to prepare the
necessary adjusting entries for items that do not meet the capitalization
threshold for PPE. Henceforth, the purchase of supplies that are small enough
to be considered PPE shall be recorded by debiting the appropriate inventory
account. In recording the corresponding expense accounts, the basis of the
Municipal Accountant shall be the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued
(SSMI) and the original copy of the Requisition and Issuance Slip (RIS) from the
Municipal Treasurer or designated Supply Officer.

We recommended that Management install internal control mechanisms
by using the RIS to request and issue supplies and materials carried in stock and
consolidate the same in the RSMI, which shall be submitted to the Municipal
Accountant to record the appropriate expense accounts in the books for the
supplies consumed.

We further recommended that Management direct the Municipal
Accountant to stop the practice of recording purchases of supplies and materials
as outright expenses and henceforth record purchases and utilization of supplies
and materials in accordance with Section 114 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs,
Volume I and Section IV of COA Circular No. 2014-002, dated April 15, 2014,
to fairly present the expense and inventory accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to locate the
relevant documents to serve as the basis for analyzing prior years’ reconciling
items and preparing the necessary adjustments, if any. Henceforth, exercise
care in recording and classifying the Municipality’s financial transactions in
accordance with COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 15, 2015, to
ensure that balances of reciprocal accounts are reconciled.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the end-users to
submit PRs to the BAC for COVID-19 related items enough to fill the
Municipality’s three-month requirement except when circumstances or the
necessities of the service would demand otherwise and strictly comply with the
pertinent provisions of R.A. No. 9184, its IRR, and related issuances on the
procurement thereof.

We further recommended and the BAC Chairman agreed to observe the
judicious application of the negotiated procurement through emergency
purchase modality only when the conditions provided under Section 53.2 of the
Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 is established.

We recommended and Management agreed to submit an explanation for
the issuance of the Notice of Award following the supplier’s delivery of services,
contrary to the procurement procedures provided under Item V.D.2 (b), Annex
H of the Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184.

We further recommended and Management agreed to ensure that the
officials involved in the procurement process strictly adhere to the procedures
for Negotiated Procurement through Emergency Cases to safeguard the
government’s interests.

Furthermore, we recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to
ensure that all payments for goods and services be made by check directly to
suppliers/dealers/creditors, except for petty cash fund disbursements, in
accordance with Section 93 of P.D. No. 1445.

We recommended and Management agreed to submit to the Audit Team
a valid Permit to Purchase and a Dealer’s Permit to Sell from the NTC in order
for the transaction of purchasing handheld radios to be considered a valid and
legitimate expenditure.

Henceforth, we recommended and Management agreed to comply with
NTC Memorandum Circular No. 09-08-91 on the guidelines for the sale and
purchase of radio communications equipment to avoid disallowance in the audit.

We recommended and the LDRRMO agreed to coordinate with the OCD
on the preparation and submission of the Municipality’s PSCP pursuant to
NDRRMC Memorandum No 33, s. 2018.

We further recommended and the Local Chief Executive (LCE) agreed to
institutionalize the PSCP by creating a working group that will perform the
following activities:
a. To conduct risk assessment;
b. To identify critical processes and functions;
c. To determine scenarios that may disrupt normal operation;
d. To conduct risk analysis and impact analysis;
e. To formulate the PSCP based on the identified risks on critical
processes and functions and its related steps to be followed to
eliminate, if not mitigate, the impact of the determined
disruptions following the prescribed templates and
requirements in the PSCP guidebook; and
f. To submit plan to the MDRRMO for review, to the Local Chief
Executive for approval, and to the Sangguniang Bayan for the
passage of a resolution to institutionalize the PSCP.

We recommended and Management agreed to strictly observe the
documentary requirements on fund transfers in accordance with Section 3.1 of COA
Circular No. 2012-001 and require the MILHZ to furnish the LGU District Health
Board a copy of the financial statement including a narrative report on the
utilization of the revolving fund as required under Item III of the MOA.

We recommended and Management agreed to immediately submit to the
DILG Regional Office No. VII the required written report on the subject
acquisition, which shall contain the details regarding the type, brand, engine
displacement, quantity, price and seller of the purchased vehicle.

We further recommended and Management agreed to strictly comply with
the existing rules and regulations on the purchase/acquisition of government
motor vehicles.

We recommended that the officials and personnel involved in said
procurement submit an explanation why they should not be held liable for the
loss to the government amounting to ₱78,500.00 arising from the purchase of
COVID-19 rapid antigen test kits exceeding the ceiling price provided under the
DOH Department Circular No. 2021-0323 dated August 6, 2021. We further
recommended that, henceforth, officials involved in the procurement of goods
and services comply with laws and regulations, particularly R.A. No. 7581 or the
“Price Act,” as amended, to protect the interest of the government.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor submit a justification on the
necessity of procuring a high-end or expensive brand/model mobile phone,
which is considered “unnecessary” under Annex C of COA Circular No. 2012-
003 dated October 29, 2012, otherwise, restitute the value of the mobile phone.

We further recommended and Management agreed to stop referring to
brand names in preparing PRs. Instead, mention the relevant specifications
and/or performance requirements of the goods requested. Henceforth, we
recommended and Management agreed to comply with the procurement
procedures for SVP provided under the pertinent provisions of the Revised IRR
of R.A. No. 9184.

We recommended that the MSWDO in the distribution of relief goods, Management prepare the RDS, which should contain the name and signature of recipients as well as the date of receipt, and such other necessary information, including the kind and quantity of items received to establish accountability.
observations.managementAction: The MSWDO ensured that the RDS was completely complied in the distribution of relief goods.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the BAC, through its Secretariat, to observe the provisions of GPPB Circular No. 01-2020 dated April 6, 2020 in the posting in the GPPB online portal the APP, NOA, and other information relative to the procurement of projects. We also recommended that the BAC Secretariat send a written notice to COA on the posting of NOA, Contract or Purchase Orders and other necessary information in any conspicuous place in the premises of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The BAC Chairman informed the Audit Team that the Municipality had registered its account in the GPPB online portal on posting in January 27, 2021. Shortly after the enrollment of its account in the portal, the BAC submitted to the GPPB the necessary documentary requirements for posting.
observations.reason: The GPPB online portal was still not updated. The latest posting of APP, NOAs, and other information about goods procured through emergency procurement was in CY 2020.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management utilize the LDRRMF-STF in accordance with Section 5.0 of the NDRRMC, DBM and DILG JMC No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013 and the pertinent provisions of DBM Local Budget Circular No. 124 dated March 26, 2020.
observations.managementAction: A daily food allowance of ₱80.00 to ₱100.00 per meal was no longer granted out of the LDRRMF-STF.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management stop the practice of procuring through reimbursement and strictly observe the pertinent provisions of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 and relevant GPPB issuances to ensure transparency, economy, and efficiency in the procurement process.
observations.managementAction: Management no longer procure goods on a reimbursement basis upon receipt of the audit observation.
observations.reason: There were transactions in the first quarter of CY 2021 on the procurement of meals for PUMs and LSIs, which were paid through reimbursement.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the monthly BRS for all bank accounts for CY 2019 and immediately prepare correcting/adjusting entries for any discrepancies, errors or other reconciling items noted to reflect the correct balance of the cash account in the financial statements. We also recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to, henceforth, submit the BRS, including bank statements, paid checks, copies of debit and credit memos, and subsidiary ledgers, to the Auditor within the prescribed timeframe.
observations.managementAction: Bank printouts were requested from the depository banks instead of waiting for the bank to send the bank statement to the LGU. Moreover, complete bank statements and subsidiary ledgers were not attached to the BRS.
observations.reason: The monthly BRS for CY 2021 was not promptly submitted to the Audit Team. On June 3, 2022, the Municipal Accountant only submitted the BRS for the month of December.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management enjoin the concerned department heads to strictly comply with the accounting and reporting guidelines on local roads asset management system, and to earnestly perform their respective duties and responsibilities set forth under COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated October 23, 2015. We further recommended that Management create an Inventory Committee to conduct an annual physical count of all LRN and the results thereof reported in the RPCLRN to be submitted to the Auditor and Accounting unit not later than January 31 of each year. The RPCLRN shall be reconciled with the accounting records and the necessary adjusting entries shall be prepared before the financial statement date.
observations.managementAction: An Inventory Committee had already been created by Management.
observations.reason: The RPCLRN showing the result of the physical count was not submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management adhere to Section 313 of R.A. No. 7160 and the pertinent provisions of DBM Local Budget Circular No. 111 dated June 10, 2016 on the maintenance of special accounts and AOB for each LEE. Henceforth, maintain separate books of accounts as well as prepare the financial statements for each of the economic enterprises in accordance with Sections 107 and 110 of the NGAS Manual, Volume I, to provide Management with relevant financial information needed for decision and policy making.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant had already maintained separate books of accounts for each LEE.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management comply with the legal requirements needed in obtaining the transfer of land title in favor of the Municipality and submit the aforementioned lacking documents as required under Section 13.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 to enable proper evaluation of the propriety of the claim.
observations.managementAction: On a test basis, post audit of DVs relative to the purchase of a lot disclosed that this was already supported with TCT in the name of the Municipality. However, Management has not submitted to the Auditor the lacking documents on the subject transaction.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the MSWDO ensure the SEF is appropriated only for the priority/allowable expenditures pursuant to AO No. 04 dated February 23, 2016. Henceforth, review the PPAs included in the SEF budget to ensure proper utilization thereof, and for the Municipal Accountant to secure from the DepEd Accountant a certification that the request for the use of SEF is net of the budgetary provision for the same or related item(s) in the budget of the DepEd and the ECCD Council. We further recommended that Management request from DepEd the reimbursement of the registration fees incurred during the 2019 NSPC in accordance with Section 4 of DepEd Memorandum No. 197 s. 2018 dated December 27, 2018 as well as traveling expenses incurred by DepEd personnel during their attendance to seminar workshops pursuant to Section 24 of Executive Order No. 77 dated March 15, 2019.
observations.managementAction: The Master Teacher informed the Audit Team that DepEd-Division of Negros Oriental will reimburse the registration fees incurred during the 2019 NSPC.
observations.reason: Proof of receipt for the reimbursement was not submitted to the Auditor for validation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant require all the PHWs and Agricultural Technologists to submit proof of actual exposure to specific health hazards and occupational risks, and documents required under Section 5.8 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 to support their hazard pay. Henceforth, pay hazard pay based on the actual days of exposure to risks or danger in accordance with Section 7 of the Revised IRR of R.A. No. 7305.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant has already demanded collection from the concerned officials.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that in the planning phase, the Municipal Engineer, in coordination with the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator, review the feasibility of implementation of the identified PPAs under the 20% DF with corresponding quantifiable targets which can be implemented within the desired time frame. We also recommended that in the preparation of the Annual Procurement Plan (APP) and Project Procurement Management Plan (PPMP), the implementing unit shall determine whether infrastructure projects will be contracted out or implemented by administration in accordance with the guidelines issued by the GPPB to maximize the utilization of the 20% IRA. Henceforth, exhaust all means to implement without further delay the projects to maximize the use of the fund to achieve the desired socio-economic development and environmental outcomes pursuant to Section 5.0 of DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017.
observations.managementAction:

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management optimally utilize the 70% LDRRMF for its intended purpose and for expenditures that would strengthen its capacity to avoid or mitigate the adverse effects of disasters or calamities. Expenditures which are administrative in nature shall be properly charged against the regular budget or funds of the Municipality.
observations.reason: There were some expenditures which are not properly chargeable to the LDRRMF.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that MSWDO submit the aforementioned documents in order for us to properly evaluate the utilization of the SFP Fund and to ascertain whether the objectives of the program were attained pursuant to AO No. 04 dated February 23, 2016. Henceforth, maintain stock cards to record supplies relative to the implementation of the SFP.
observations.managementAction: Acknowledgement receipts were submitted by the MSWDO to the Audit Team on April 24, 2019.
observations.reason: Examination disclosed that the distribution lists or AR for the following procurements were not included: Voucher No. 401-18-02-0055 02/23/18 Verlyn’s Enterprises ₱255,897.40; 401-18-04-0135 04/17/18 Verlyn’s Enterprises ₱255,643.80; 401-18-06-0240 06/11/18 Verlyn’s Enterprises ₱255,897.40. Total ₱767,435.60

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the MPDO to exhaust all available resources for the collection and processing of data requirements necessary for the establishment of the GAD database as storage of effective gender-responsive planning, programming, and policy formulation in accordance with PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-1 to ensure that the PPAs are aligned with GAD-related undertakings so that the intended beneficiaries are assured of the services, protection, and other assistance that may be provided.
observations.reason: The GAD database was still not fully established.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management strictly observe and implement the regulations on the grant, utilization, and liquidation of cash advances pursuant to COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997. We further recommended that management demand from concerned officials and employees the immediate settlement of their cash advances.
observations.managementAction: Management demand from the concerned officials and employees the immediate settlement of their cash advances.
observations.reason: Advances to Officers and Employees amounting to ₱501,500.00 as of December 31, 2021 remained outstanding (General Fund ₱1,500.00 Over one year; Trust Fund ₱500,000.00 More than three years).

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management prioritize the allocation of funds for the establishment and operation of a sanitary landfill in accordance with Sections 40 and 41 of RA 9003. We also recommended that the MWMB ensure the implementation of the Solid Waste Management Plan of the Municipality and enjoin the Barangays to actively participate in the implementation of waste reduction and segregation at source.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality had already allocated the amount of ₱13 million from the 20% DF for the Solid Waste Facility on top of the plastic shredding/grinding machine, mortar mixer and plastic melting densifier machine. Likewise, the LGU has implemented barangay-based garbage collections.
observations.reason: The SWMB still had to finalize the SWMP in coordination with the DENR to establish the sanitary landfill.

observations.recommendation: We required the agency head to strictly implement the policies governing the settlement of disallowance and issuance of clearance. As much as practicable, resort to some remedies and sanctions to effect settlement, such as salary deductions to be applied in satisfaction of the indebtedness to the government. Send written notices to the officials and employees who are no longer in the service, and determine the liability of officials who issued clearance despite the existence of unsettled disallowances.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant has already demanded collection from the concerned officials.
observations.reason: There were still disallowances which remained unsettled.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management adhere with Section 313 of R.A. No. 7160 and the pertinent provisions of DBM Local Budget Circular No. 111 dated June 10, 2016 on the maintenance of special accounts and AOB for each economic enterprise. Henceforth, the Municipal Accountant maintain separate books of accounts as well as prepare the financial statements for each of the economic enterprise in accordance with Sections 107 and 110 of the NGAS Manual, Volume I, to provide Management with relevant financial information needed for decision and policy making.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2019 pending the approval of the Sangguniang through a resolution on the creation of a special account for each economic enterprise (LEE).
observations.reason: A special account for each LEE of the Municipality was not prepared pending the approval of the Sangguniang through a resolution on the creation of a special account for each economic enterprise.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant provide disclosures on the face of the SCBAA and/or the Notes to FS in the SCBAA on the changes between the original and final budgets and reconciliation of actual amounts per SCBAA and per financial statements, to enhance the transparency of the Municipality’s financial statements. Likewise, adequate disclosure shall be provided in the Notes to FS for the details of the Loans Payable pursuant to paragraph 127 of PPSAS No. 1. We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer reconcile monthly their respective records to avoid the accumulation of reconciling items and to facilitate the reconciliation of balances between the SCBAA and FS.
observations.reason: Examination of the NFS disclosed that an explanation on the changes between the original and final budget in the SCBAA as well as the reconciliation of actual amounts against the Statement of Financial Performance were not disclosed. Likewise, there was inadequate disclosure on long-term liabilities.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer provide the Municipal Accountant a copy of the RPT records for CY 2018 and prior years to establish the beginning balance for CY 2019. Henceforth, furnish the Municipal Accountant at the beginning of each year a certified list of taxpayers with the amount due and collectible for the current year as basis for recording the RPT and SET Receivables in compliance with Section 20 of the NGAS Manual, Volume I.
observations.reason: The setting up of RPT and SET Receivables were still not based on a certified list of taxpayers.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LDRRM Council pass a resolution reverting the CYs 2011 to 2013 LDRRMF unspent balances to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund, for adoption by the Sangguniang Bayan thru the Local Development Council. We further recommended that the members of the Local Finance Committee prepare the necessary transfer of funds and accounting reports to facilitate the said reversion and certify to the availability of said funds for subsequent reappropriation for social services.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2018 No reversion was made.
observations.reason: Examination of the Schedule of Trust Liabilities-Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund as of December 31, 2020 disclosed that the unexpended balances for CYs 2014 and 2015 amounting to ₱1,758,238.94 and ₱1,948,543.80, respectively, were still not reverted to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management ensure that the PPAs are aligned with GAD-related undertakings so that the intended beneficiaries are assured of the services, protection and other assistance that may be provided, in order to attain the objective for which the fund was created.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2018 No sufficient actions taken to ensure all PPAs charged to GAD were GAD-compliant.
observations.reason: There were still ineligible expenses charged to GAD such as social pension of senior citizens as well as medical and hospitalization subsidy with a total appropriation of ₱5,000,000.00.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant coordinate with the MPDC and MSWDO in tracing and locating documents to support the claims. We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant issue demand letters to concerned individuals, determine its collectability and, if warranted, request for write-off from the Commission on Audit.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2017 No tracing and formal demand letters have been fully executed.
observations.reason: As of December 31, 2020, the accounts remained dormant and no authority to write-off was requested from COA. Examination of the schedules disclosed that the amount of ₱1,250,060.00 is still subject for identification.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant see to it that: (a) the grant of initial cash advance for the PCF is supported with authority by the Local Chief Executive and the estimate of expenses; (b) replenishment of the cash advance is made only when the disbursements reach at least 75%, or as the need requires, by submitting a replenishment voucher supported by PCVs and other documents duly summarized in a report of disbursement; and (c) the PCF is not used for regular expenses, such as rentals, subscription, utilities and the like.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2017 Management to ensure the PCF is used only for authorized purposes and replenish accordingly.
observations.reason: At the end of the year, the petty cash fund shall be fully liquidated by preparing Report of Disbursement supported by the list/summary of PCVs, the PCVs and its supporting documents. A balance of ₱8,417.27 was still not liquidated and some documentary requirements on the granting of PCF were not attached to the DV.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management ensure that the LCPC performs its functions in accordance with DILG MC No. 2005-07 dated February 1, 2005.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2017 No formal records of meetings and accomplishments were submitted to COA.
observations.reason: The LCPC did not submit the Records or Minutes of Meetings and Accomplishment Report, which are indicators that the LCPC is operational or functional; hence, we could not validate whether it is fully operational.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Inventory Committee to conduct the physical count of assets annually. We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer reconcile their records with the results of the actual count.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2016 No physical count was conducted.
observations.reason: Physical count was not conducted in CY 2020 due to COVID-19 pandemic.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management stop the payment and require the concerned BIR personnel, including the approving officials, to refund the honoraria received for lack of legal basis. We further recommended that a Notice of Disallowance be issued if necessary.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2016 The Municipal Accountant demanded refund from the concerned BIR personnel and the approving officials.
observations.reason: The Notice of Disallowance remained unsettled as of December 31, 2020 despite the demand made by the Municipal Accountant from the BIR personnel.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the local chief executive, together with the Sanggunian, require the refund of the excess amount granted and discontinue the granting of PEI in excess of the allowable maximum rate of ₱5,000.00 per personnel, as provided for in Section 1.a of E.O. No. 80 dated July 20, 2012 to avoid overpayment.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2014 The agency head was required to refund in full the amounts received in excess of what is authorized in DBM Budget Circular No. 2010-3 totaling ₱1,260,000.00, otherwise, a Notice of Disallowance shall be issued in this regard.
observations.reason: An appeal was submitted to COA Regional Office No. VII.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Inventory Committee include the count of supplies and materials when it conducts its actual physical count and for the Accounting and Municipal Treasurer to maintain required supplies ledger cards and stock cards, respectively, to comply with the prescribed procedures in the preparation of the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) so that utilization can be recorded appropriately in the books. Furthermore, the Municipal Accountant shall strictly follow the Perpetual Method of recording its inventory and recognize inventory accounts for the above supplies immediately recorded as expense.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2014 No full compliance due to manpower and storage constraints.
observations.reason: Supplies and materials were still recorded as direct expense because of the lack of manpower to handle the account and unavailability of storage room.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to prepare and
take up the necessary adjusting entries in the books and debit to “Due from
Local Government Units” account fund transfers to local government units in
accordance with Section 5.1 of COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1,
2015.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the recipient
barangays to liquidate the funds by submitting the documentary requirements
prescribed under Section 3.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14,
2012, and comply with the documentary requirements on fund transfers.

We therefore recommended that Management continue to
ensure that essential emergency medicines are procured at the most advantageous
price for the government based on the existing data of DOH or other related
agencies in accordance with Section 5 of GPPB Circular No. 2020-001 dated
April 6, 2020.

We further recommended that Management require the concerned personnel or the
end-user duly authorized by the Head of the Procuring Entity to completely and
properly fill up the relevant and required information in the PO pursuant to Item B
of COA Circular No. 96-010 dated August 15, 1996.

We recommended and the MSWDO agreed that in the distribution of
relief goods, Management prepare the RDS which should contain the name and
signature of recipients as well as the date of receipt, and such other necessary
information, including the kind and quantity of items received to establish
accountability.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the BAC, through its
Secretariat, to observe the provisions of GPPB Circular No. 01-2020 dated April 6,
2020 in the posting in the GPPB online portal the updated APP, NOA and other
information relative to the procurement of projects. We also recommended that the
BAC Secretariat send a written notice to COA on the posting of NOA, Contract or
Purchase Orders and other necessary information in any conspicuous place in the
premises of the Municipality.

We recommended that Management utilize the LDRRMF-STF in accordance
with Section 5.0 of the NDRRMC, DBM and DILG JMC No. 2013-1 dated March
25, 2013 and the pertinent provisions of DBM Local Budget Circular No. 124 dated
March 26, 2020.

We recommended and Management agreed to stop the practice of
procurement through reimbursement and strictly observe the pertinent provisions of
the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 and relevant GPPB issuances to ensure
transparency, economy and efficiency in the procurement process.

We recommended that Management submit the Authority for the
Entitlement of the Hazard Pay in accordance with Section 4.3 DBM Budget Circular
No. 2020-1 dated March 24, 2020 for us to ascertain the propriety and validity of the
claim. Further, we recommended that Management require the refund of the daily
hazard pay granted to seven personnel who did not physically report for work on
certain dates during the quarantine period.

We reiterated our audit recommendation and the Municipal Accountant
agreed to require the Agricultural Technologists to submit proof of actual
exposure to specific hazards and occupational risks, in accordance with DBM-
DOH Joint Circular No. 1 series of 2016. We further recommended that
Management submit to the Auditor the Certification from the Department of
Health to attest that the Agricultural Technologists are considered PHWs within
the contemplation of RA No. 7305.

We recommended that Management prepare a one-time report to the
National Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council, through the Office
of the Civil Defense on all donations received by the Municipality and furnish
the Auditor a copy of the (1) Acknowledgement Receipts of the Donations In-
Kind; (2) Proof of receipt by and distribution to the beneficiaries; and (3)
Inventory of remaining undistributed items, if any, in accordance with Section 1
of COA Circular No. 2020-009 dated April 21, 2020.

We recommended and Management agreed to refrain from procuring
goods thru reimbursement and to make payments by check directly to
suppliers in order to withhold the taxes due the government pursuant to the
pertinent provisions of Revenue Regulations Nos. 2-98 and 16-2005 dated April
17, 1998 and September 1, 2005, respectively.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the monthly BRS for all bank accounts for CY 2019 and to immediately prepare correcting/adjusting entries for any discrepancies/errors or other reconciling items noted to reflect the correct balance of the cash account in the financial statements. We also recommended verification of the validity and correctness of the cash in bank balances could not including bank statements, paid checks, copies of debit/credit memos and subsidiary ledgers to the Auditor within the prescribed timeframe.
observations.managementAction: Bank printouts were requested from the depository banks instead of waiting for the bank to send the bank statement to the LGU. On May 11, 2021, out of the eight accounts, the Municipal Accountant submitted the BRS of four accounts for the year ended December 31, 2020.
observations.reason: The bank statements and subsidiary ledgers were not attached to the BRS submitted by the Municipal Accountant. Moreover, correcting/adjusting entries for the discrepancies/errors or other reconciling items amounting to ₱1,049,374.00 were not prepared.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management enjoin the concerned department heads to strictly comply with the accounting and reporting guidelines on local roads management system, and to earnestly perform their respective duties and responsibilities set forth under COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated October 23, 2015. We further recommended that Management create an Inventory Committee to conduct an annual physical count of all LRN and the results thereof reported in the RPCLRN to be submitted to the Auditor and Accounting unit not later than January 31 of each year. The RPCLRN shall be reconciled with the accounting records and the necessary adjusting entries be prepared before the statement date.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2019 Management created the Inventory Committee.
observations.reason: However, the RPCLRN was not prepared because no physical count was conducted during the year because of the COVID-19 pandemic. As a result, reconciliation of the results of the counts and the accounting records was not made.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management consolidate the requisition of catering services and stop the practice of acquiring goods and services on a staggered basis thru personal canvass. We further recommended that the BAC undertake the procurement through competitive bidding in accordance with Section 10, Rule IV of the IRR of R.A. No. 9184, except as provided in Rule XVI of the IRR.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2019 Due to COVID-19 pandemic, Management resorted to negotiated procurement. Moreover, because of the high demand of supplies, some suppliers could not provide the total quantity required at the time need.
observations.reason: Due to COVID-19 pandemic, Management resorted to negotiated procurement. Moreover, because of the high demand of supplies, some suppliers could not provide the total quantity required at the time need.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management comply with the legal requirements needed in obtaining the transfer of land title in favor of the Municipality Sibulan and submit the aforementioned lacking documents as required under Section 13.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 to enable proper evaluation. Henceforth, strict compliance with the requirements for purchase of parcels of land in accordance with Section 4.6 of PD No. 1445 and Section 449, Volume I of GAAM.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2019 Management submitted the TCT in the name of the Municipality.
observations.reason: However, Management has not submitted to the Auditor the lacking documents on the subject transaction.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal School Board ensure that SEF are appropriated only for the priority/allowable expenses enumerated under Section 272 of RA No. 7160 and its related DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, review the PPAs included in the SEF budget to ensure proper utilization thereof, and for the Municipal Accountant to secure from the DepEd Accountant a certification that request on the use of SEF is net of the budgetary provision for the same or related item(s) in the budget of the DepEd and the ECCD Council. We further recommended that Management request from DepEd the reimbursement of the registration fees incurred during the 2019 NSPC in accordance with Section 4 of DepEd Memorandum No. 197 s. 2018 dated December 27, 2018 as well as the traveling expenses incurred by DepEd personnel during their attendance to seminar workshops pursuant to Section 24 of Executive Order No. 77 dated March 15, 2019 in order that the funds of the Municipality may be restored.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2019 On a test basis, post audit of DVs relative to the purchase disclosed that this was already supported with TCT in the name of the Municipality. Management informed that DepEd-Division of Negros Oriental will reimburse the registration fees incurred during the 2019 NSPC; however, proof of receipt on the reimbursement was not submitted to the Auditor for validation.
observations.reason: Although DepEd will reimburse the registration fees, proof of receipt on the reimbursement was not submitted to the Auditor for validation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant require all the PHWs and Agricultural Technologists to submit proof of actual exposure to specific health hazards and occupational risks, and documents required under Section 5.8 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 to support their hazard pay. Henceforth, we recommended that Management pay hazard pay based on the actual days of exposure to risks or danger in accordance with Section 7 of the Revised IRR of R.A. No. 7305.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2019 Management submitted the required documents for some PHWs. Hazard pay for the period July to December 2020 were no longer granted to the Agricultural Technologists pending the submission of the certification from DOH.
observations.reason: Although some documents were submitted, hazard pay was still paid to some personnel for certain periods and the Certification from DOH for Agricultural Technologists was still pending.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management comply with the rules and regulations on the grant of Uniform/Clothing Allowance, whether in cash or in kind, and desist from providing uniforms to JO personnel in accordance with Budget Circular No. 2018-1 dated March 8, 2018 to avoid incurrence of irregular expenditures.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2019 Management no longer procured rescue uniforms for JO personnel.
observations.reason: The Municipality no longer procured rescue uniforms and the regular employee who already received the clothing allowance was not given duplicate benefits.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that in the planning phase, the Municipal Engineer in coordination with Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator, review the feasibility of implementation of the identified PPAs under the 20% DF with corresponding quantifiable targets which can be implemented within the desired time frame. We also recommended that in the preparation of the Annual Procurement Plan (APP) and Project Procurement Management Plan (PPMP), the implementing unit shall determine whether infrastructure projects will be contracted out or implemented by administration in accordance with the guidelines issued by the GPPB to maximize the utilization of the 20% IRA. Henceforth, exhaust all means to implement without further delay the projects to maximize the use of the fund to achieve the desired socio-economic development and environmental outcomes pursuant to Section 5.0 of DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management optimally utilize 70% LDRRMF for its intended purpose and for Management strengthen its capacity to avoid or mitigate the adverse effects of disasters or calamity. Expenditures which are administrative in nature shall be properly charged against the regular budget or funds of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2018 On a test basis, post-audit of the DVs disclosed that there were still inappropriate expenses charged to LDRRMF, such as repairs and maintenance of rescue vehicles.
observations.reason: Expenditures which are administrative in nature were still charged to LDRRMF; such expenditures should be charged to the regular budget.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that MSWDO submit the aforementioned documents in order for us to properly evaluate the utilization of the SFP Fund and to ascertain whether the objectives of the program were attained pursuant to AO No. 04 dated February 23, 2016. Henceforth, require the MSWDO to maintain stock cards to record issuances of supplies relative to the implementation of the SFP.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2018 Acknowledgement receipts were submitted by the MSWDO to the Audit Team on April 24, 2019. Management informed that there was no supplementary feeding in CY 2020 because of the pandemic. Henceforth, the MSWDO shall maintain stock cards and submit supporting documents.
observations.reason: Review of the acknowledgement receipts on the distribution of the ingredients for the SFP to the 23 DCCs disclosed that some of the procurement vouchers were not included in the distribution lists or acknowledgement receipts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the MPDO to exhaust all available resources for the collection and processing of data requirements of the GAD database as necessary information in aid of effective gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation in accordance with PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-1 to ensure that the PPAs are aligned with GAD-related undertakings so that the intended beneficiaries are assured of the services, protection, and other assistance that may be provided.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2017 Management initiated actions to compile GAD related data and align PPAs accordingly.
observations.reason: GAD database was still not fully established.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management strictly observe and implement the regulations on the grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advances pursuant to COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997. We further recommended that management demand from concerned officials/employees the immediate settlement of their cash advances.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2017 Management advised concerned officers to settle their advances and recovery actions were initiated.
observations.reason: There were still unliquidated advances to officers and employees amounting to ₱501,780.00 as of December 31, 2020, summarized as: General Fund ₱1,780.00 (91 days to over one year) and Trust Fund ₱500,000.00 (More than three years). Total ₱501,780.00.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management prioritize the allocation of funds for the establishment and operation of a sanitary landfill in accordance with Sections 40 and 41 of RA 9003. We recommended further that the MWMB ensure the implementation of the Solid Waste Management Plan of the Municipality and enjoin the Barangays to actively participate in the implementation of waste reduction and segregation at source.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2017 The Municipality had already allocated the amount of ₱13 million from the 20% DF for the Solid Waste Management Treatment Facility on top of the plastic shredding/grinding machine, mortar mixer and plasting melting densifier machine. The LGU had implemented the barangay-based garbage collections.
observations.reason: The sanitary landfill was still not prepared. However, the LGU implemented barangay-based garbage collection and allocated funds for SWM equipment.

observations.recommendation: We recommended immediate require all collecting officers including all field collectors to maintain and update the entries in the cashbooks where all their transactions will be recorded pursuant to the above-cited provisions on the Handbook on Cash Examination. Effect regular quarterly reconciliation of the cashbooks to be maintained by the collecting officers and the subsidiary ledgers kept by the Accounting Unit.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2012 Collecting officers already maintained cashbooks and reconciliation was regularly made against the subsidiary ledgers of the Accounting Unit.
observations.reason: Collecting officers already maintained cashbooks and reconciliation was regularly made against the subsidiary ledgers of the Accounting Unit.

observations.recommendation: We required the agency head to refund in full the amounts received in excess of what is authorized in DBM Budget Circular No. 2010-3 totaling ₱1,260,000.00, otherwise, a Notice of Disallowance shall be issued in this regard. Henceforth, we also enjoin the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Budget Officer to be cognizant of the pertinent provisions of law on the grant of allowances/incentives and not to certify disbursements which run counter to the intents and purposes of the regulations prescribed by the appropriate and concerned government agency such as the Department of Budget and Management.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2011 The Municipal Accountant demanded collection from concerned officials.
observations.reason: An appeal was submitted to COA Regional Office No. VII.

observations.recommendation: We required the agency head to strictly implement the policies governing the settlement of disallowance and issuance of clearance. As much as practicable, resort to some remedies and sanctions to effect settlement, such as salary deductions to be applied in satisfaction of the indebtedness to the government. Send written notices to the officials and employees who are no longer in the service, and determine the liability of officials who issued clearance despite the existence of unsettled disallowances.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2008 The Municipal Accountant demanded the agency head to implement the policies and resort to remedies to collect the unsettled disallowances. Recovery actions were initiated.
observations.reason: There were still disallowances which remained unsettled.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant
to prepare the monthly BRS for all bank accounts for CY 2019 and to immediately
prepare correcting/adjusting entries for any discrepancies/errors or other reconciling
items noted to reflect the correct balance of the cash account in the financial
statements. We also recommended and the Municipal Accountatnt, henceforth,
submit the BRS including bank statements, paid checks, copies of debit/credit memos
and subsidiary ledgers to the Auditor within the prescribed timeframe.

We recommended and Management agreed to enjoin the concerned
department heads to strictly comply with the accounting and reporting
guidelines on local roads asset management system, and to earnestly perform
their respective duties and responsibilities set forth under COA Circular No.
2015-008 dated October 23, 2015.

We further recommended and Management agreed to create an
Inventory Committee to conduct an annual physical count of all LRN and the
results thereof reported in the RPCLRN to be submitted to the Auditor and
Accounting unit not later than January 31 of each year. The RPCLRN shall be
reconciled with the accounting records and the necessary adjusting entries be
prepared before the statement date.

We recommended that Management adhere with Section 313 of R.A.
No. 7160 and the pertinent provisions of DBM Local Budget Circular No. 111 dated
June 10, 2016 on the maintenance of special accounts and AOB for each LEE.
Henceforth, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant maintain
separate books of accounts as well as prepare the financial statements for each of
the economic enterprise in accordance with Sections 107 and 110 of the NGAS
Manual, Volume I, to provide Management with relevant financial information
needed for decision and policy making.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to provide
disclosures on the face of the SCBAA and/or the Notes to FS the changes
between the original and final budgets and reconciliation of actual amounts per
SCBAA and per financial statements, to enhance the transparency of the
Municipality’s financial statements. Likewise, adequate disclosure shall be
provided in the Notes to FS for the details of the Loans Payable pursuant to
paragraph 127 of PPSAS No. 1.

We further recommended and the Municipal Accountant and Municipal
Budget Officer agreed to reconcile monthly their respective records to avoid the
accumulation of reconciling items and to facilitate the reconciliation of balances
between the SCBAA and FS.

We recommended that Management consolidate the requisition of
catering services and stop the practice of acquiring goods and services on a
staggered basis thru personal canvass. We further recommended that the BAC
undertake the procurement through competitive bidding in accordance with
Section 10, Rule IV of the of the IRR of R.A. No. 9184, except as provided in
Rule XVI of the IRR.

We recommended that Management comply with the legal requirements
needed in obtaining the transfer of land title in favor of the Municipality Sibulan
and submit the aforementioned lacking documents as required under Section
13.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 to enable us to properly evaluate the
propriety of the claim.

Henceforth, we recommended strict compliance with the requirements
for purchase of parcels of land in accordance with Section 4.6 of PD No. 1445
and Section 449, Volume I of GAAM.

We recommended and the Municipal School Board agreed to ensure that
SEF are appropriated only for the priority/allowable expenses enumerated
under Section 272 of RA No. 7160 and its related DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint
Circular No. 1, review the PPAs included in the SEF budget to ensure proper
utilization thereof, and for the Municipal Accountant to secure from the DepEd
Accountant a certification that request on the use of SEF is net of the budgetary
provision for the same or related item(s) in the budget of the DepEd and the
ECCD Council.

Likewise, we recommended that Management request from DepEd the
reimbursement of the registration fees incurred during the 2019 NSPC in
accordance with Section 4 of DepEd Memorandum No. 197 s. 2018 dated
December 27, 2018 as well as the traveling expenses incurred by DepEd
personnel during their attendance to seminar workshops pursuant to Section 24
of Executive Order No. 77 dated March 15, 2019 in order that the funds of the
Municipality may be restored.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant require all the PHWs
and Agricultural Technologists to submit proof of actual exposure to specific health
hazards and occupational risks, and documents required under Section 5.8 of COA
Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 to support their hazard pay. Henceforth,
we recommended that Management pay hazard pay based on the actual days of
exposure to risks or danger in accordance with Section 7 of the Revised IRR of R.A.
No. 7305.

We recommended and Management agreed to comply with the rules and
regulations on the grant of Uniform/Clothing Allowance, whether in cash or in
kind, and desist from providing uniforms to JO personnel in accordance with
Budget Circular No. 2018-1 dated March 8, 2018 to avoid incurrence of
irregular expenditures.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant coordinate with the MPDC and MSWDO in tracing and locating documents to support the claims. We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant issue demand letters to concerned individuals, determine its collectability and, if warranted, request for write-off from the Commission on Audit.
observations.reason: The accounts remained dormant and no authority to write-off was requested from COA.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management, henceforth, desist from using the LDRRMF for expenses which are not disaster-related and strictly use the fund in accordance with the provisions of Republic Act No. 10121 and NDRMC, DBM and DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013.
observations.reason: Expenditures which were not disaster related were still charged to the LDRRMF.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the local chief executive, together with the Sanggunian, require the refund of the excess amount granted and discontinue the granting of PEI in excess of the allowable maximum rate of ₱5,000.00 per personnel, as provided for in Section 1.a of E.O. No. 80 dated July 20, 2012 to avoid overpayment.
observations.reason: Appeal was submitted to COA Regional Office No. VII.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Inventory Committee include the count of supplies and materials when it conducts its actual physical count and for the Accounting and Municipal Treasurer to maintain required supplies ledger cards and stock cards, respectively, to comply with the prescribed procedures in the preparation of the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) so that utilization can be recorded appropriately in the books. Furthermore, the Municipal Accountant shall strictly follow the Perpetual Method of recording its inventory, as provided in the NGAS Manual and recognize inventory accounts for the above supplies immediately recorded as expense.
observations.reason: Supplies and materials were still recorded as direct expense because of the lack of manpower to handle the account.

observations.recommendation: We required the immediate refund in full of the amounts received in excess of what is authorized in DBM Budget Circular No. 2010-3 totaling ₱1,260,000.00, otherwise, a Notice of Disallowance shall be issued in this regard. Henceforth, the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Budget Officer to be cognizant of the pertinent provisions of law on the grant of allowances/incentives and not to certify disbursements which runs counter to the intents and purposes of the regulations prescribed by appropriate and concerned government agency such as the Department of Budget and Management.
observations.reason: An appeal was submitted to COA Regional Office No. VII.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Municipal Treasurer agreed to provide the
Municipal Accountant a copy of the RPT records for CY 2018 and prior years to
establish the beginning balance for CY 2019. Henceforth, furnish the
Municipal Accountant at the beginning of each year a certified list of taxpayers
with the amount due and collectible for the current year as basis for recording
the RPT and SET Receivables in compliance with Section 20 of the NGAS
Manual, Volume 1.

We recommended that in the planning phase, the Municipal Engineer in
coordination with the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator,
review the feasibility of implementation of the identified PPAs under the 20%
DF with corresponding quantifiable targets which can be implemented within
the desired time frame. We also recommended that in the preparation of the
Annual Procurement Plan (APP) and Project Procurement Management Plan
(PPMP), the implementing unit shall determine whether infrastructure projects
will be contracted out or implemented by administration in accordance with the
guidelines issued by the GPPB to maximize the utilization of the 20% IRA.
Henceforth, exhaust all means to implement without further delay the
projects to maximize the use of the fund to achieve the desired socio-economic
development and environmental outcomes pursuant to Section 5.0 of DILG-
DBM JMC No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017.

We recommended and Management agreed to optimally utilize the 70%
LDRRMF for its intended purpose and for expenditures that would strengthen
its capacity to avoid or mitigate the adverse effects of disasters or calamity.
Expenditures which are administrative in nature shall be properly charged
against the regular budget or funds of the Municipality.

We recommended and the LDRRM Council agreed to pass a resolution
reverting the CYs 2011 to 2013 LDRRMF unspent balances to the
unappropriated surplus of the General Fund, for adoption by the Sangguniang
Bayan thru the Local Development Council. We further recommended that
the members of the Local Finance Committee prepare the necessary transfer of
funds and accounting reports to facilitate the said reversion and certify to the
availability of said funds for subsequent reappropriation for social services.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the LDRRMO to
prepare the Obligation Request (OBR) together with the disbursement
vouchers/purchase requests and/or supporting documents as soon as the
obligation was incurred and immediately forward the same to the Office of the
Budget Officer.

We also recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to draw an
accounting entry to transfer the unexpended balances of the QRF and DRRMF-
MOOE from the GF to the TF at the end of the year using the accounts
discussed in the foregoing paragraphs in compliance with the accounting
requirements under Annex C of COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommended that the MSWDO submit the aforementioned
documents in order for us to properly evaluate the utilization of the SFP Fund
and to ascertain whether the objectives of the said program were attained
pursuant to AO No. 04 dated February 23, 2016. Henceforth, require the
MSWDO to maintain stock cards to record all receipts and issuances of supplies
relative to the implementation of the SFP.

We recommended that Management direct the MPDO to exhaust all
available resources for the collection and processing of data requirements of the
GAD database as storage for necessary information in aid of effective gender-
responsive planning, programming and policy formulation in accordance with
PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-1 to ensure that the PPAs are aligned
with GAD-related undertakings so that the intended beneficiaries are assured of
the services, protection, and other assistance that may be provided.

We recommended that the LCE shall, henceforth, inform the DILG
Provincial Office in writing if there are changes in the DILG endorsed GPB, for
review, to ensure that additional PPAs are relevant to the identified gender
issues in accordance with Section 6 of PCW-DBM-DILG-NEDA JMC No. 2016-
01 dated February 12, 2016.

We recommended and Management agreed to, henceforth, ensure that the
PPAs are aligned with GAD-related undertakings so that the intended
beneficiaries are assured of the services, protection and other assistance that
may be provided, in order to attain the objective for which the fund was created.

We recommended and Management agreed to immediately stop the
payment of the employer’s share of Pag-IBIG Fund premiums in excess of what
is required by law, which is only two percent of the maximum monthly
compensation of ₱5,000.00 or P100.00 per employee pursuant to the IRR of RA
No. 9679. Further, coordinate with Pag-IBIG on the possible application of the
